pages.raw_text:
OLYMPIA. Jan. 15.-The equal
suffrage lobby, with the state presi-
dent, Mrs. DeVoe, at its head, has
its plan of campaign for the ses-
sion mapped eout. The suffragists
will attack the legislature on three
sides, and hopes to win some
thing.


The first effort viii be an at
tempt to get the legislature to sub
nit the question of an amendment
of the constitution to the people,
whereby the right of suffrage will
be granted to all persons, irrespec-
tive of Sex. To secure the submits
sion of this amendment it will re-
quire two thirds vote.


If they do not meet with success
along these lines they will try to
get the right to vote for presiden-
tial electors. A majority vote of
both houses is enough to carry
this.


If the legislature turns a deaf
ear to this proposal demand will
be made for the right of women
to vote at the primary elections.
Should this point be carried, the
suffragists feel that in a short time
they would have everything they
wanted, as it would be a simple
matter for them to have men elect
ed favorable to the cause of woman
suffrage.
